# RedwoodSDK: React 19 Metadata Tags ## Background A RedwoodSDK project is pre-installed at `/home/user/myapp`. React 19 hoists `` and `<meta>` components into the document head automatically. Use that mechanism so each page renders its own page-specific metadata, while the `Document` shell stays generic. ## Requirements Implement page server components that render `<title>` and `<meta>` tags inline. Do **not** hard-code page-specific titles in the `Document` component. ## Acceptance Criteria * Project path: /home/user/myapp * Start command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173 * Port: 5173 * Routes (all under the existing `render(Document, ...)` block): * `GET /` → final HTML `<head>` must contain `<title>Home — rwsdk` and `Home`. * `GET /about` → `` must contain `About — rwsdk` and `About`. * `GET /docs` → `` contains `Docs — rwsdk`, `Docs`.

**Implementing Server Components**

I'm currently focused on integrating React 19's automatic `<title>` and `<meta>` tag hoisting within my RedwoodJS project. The goal is to implement page server components that render these tags directly, avoiding any hardcoded solutions in the `Document` file.

**Exploring Project Structure**

My current focus is on understanding the existing RedwoodJS project at `/home/user/myapp`. I'm investigating its structure and code to see how best to implement React 19's automatic `<title>` and `<meta>` tag hoisting via page server components, rather than hardcoding them.

output.content:
export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

**Confirming File Updates**

export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

part.text:
**Finalizing component structure**

I've confirmed all metadata tags are correctly placed within the page components. React 19's automatic hoisting will manage their inclusion in the head, and the document shell is appropriately generic. This concludes the task. I will now signal completion.

input.result:
I have successfully implemented the page-specific metadata tags using React 19's hoisting mechanism. Here's what was done: 1. **Document Shell Update (`src/app/document.tsx`)**: Removed the hardcoded `` tag to allow page-specific tags to be hoisted dynamically by React. 2. **Home Page (`src/app/pages/home.tsx`)**: Updated the component to render its own `<title>Home — rwsdk` and `` along with the `

Home

` body. 3. **About Page (`src/app/pages/about.tsx`)**: Created the new page with its specific ``, description `<meta>`, and `<h1>About</h1>` content. 4. **Docs Page (`src/app/pages/docs.tsx`)**: Created the new page with its specific `<title>`, description `<meta>`, `og:image` meta property, and `<h1>Docs</h1>` content. 5. **Router Update (`src/worker.tsx`)**: Registered the new `/about` and `/docs` routes within the existing `render` block. You can now start the dev server using `npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173` and the page-specific metadata will be automatically hoisted into the `<head>` of the HTML response for each corresponding route.
